best_agent_match.title: Unchecked flashloan premium split can brick flashloan repayment

rejected_candidates.title: Any account can frontrun proxy initialization
rejected_candidates.reason: This is a generic deployment-time race on an uninitialized proxy, not a demonstrated runtime protocol bug in the reviewed codebase; no non-atomic deployment flow was evidenced.

rejected_candidates.title: No Access Control on rescueTokens Function
rejected_candidates.reason: False positive. `Pool.rescueTokens` is protected by `onlyPoolAdmin` before it reaches `PoolLogic.executeRescueTokens`.

rejected_candidates.title: Unchecked Liquidation Bonus Allows Protocol Loss
rejected_candidates.reason: This depends on privileged governance writing nonsensical reserve parameters; no permissionless exploit path was shown.

rejected_candidates.title: No Health Factor Validation When Switching eMode from Category 0
rejected_candidates.reason: The issue is user-self-inflicted and does not by itself create a protocol-level exploit or bad-debt primitive.

rejected_candidates.title: Oracle Price Manipulation Risk
rejected_candidates.reason: Generic oracle-trust concern only. The reviewed code does not show that the configured oracle source is manipulable or attacker-controlled.

rejected_candidates.title: feeToVault Can Be Set to Zero Address
rejected_candidates.reason: Not a distinct vulnerability here. The retained vault-fee finding already shows that the vault is never paid at all, regardless of recipient address.

rejected_candidates.title: No Slippage Protection in Withdraw
rejected_candidates.reason: Withdrawals are deterministic lending redemptions, not market-priced swaps, so swap-style slippage protection is not applicable.

rejected_candidates.title: Unbacked Minting Without Health Factor Check
rejected_candidates.reason: `mintUnbacked` is restricted to the trusted bridge role, and the resulting exposure is explicitly tracked via `reserve.unbacked`; this is not a permissionless exploit.

rejected_candidates.title: FlashLoan Premium Can Be Set to 100%
rejected_candidates.reason: High premiums alone are a governance choice, not a vulnerability. The retained flashloan finding covers the concrete missing split invariant that can break repayment.

rejected_candidates.title: Rebalance Stable Rate Does Not Validate New Rate
rejected_candidates.reason: Stable-rate rebalancing is intended protocol behavior and is gated by `ValidationLogic.validateRebalanceStableBorrowRate`.

rejected_candidates.title: setLendingGauge Allows Zero Address Without Validation
rejected_candidates.reason: A zero gauge is handled by the surrounding code paths and merely disables reward-allocation updates; no security impact was shown.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing Validation for Liquidation Bonus vs LTV Relationship
rejected_candidates.reason: This is another privileged configuration-hygiene concern without a concrete exploit path beyond governance setting arbitrary bad parameters.

rejected_candidates.title: Division by Zero in Health Factor Calculation
rejected_candidates.reason: False positive. The zero-collateral case is explicitly handled, and the shown code path does not divide by zero.

rejected_candidates.title: No Access Control on initialize Function After First Initialization
rejected_candidates.reason: False positive. `initializer` prevents repeated calls, and front-running `Pool.initialize` does not seize privileges or change the immutable provider.

rejected_candidates.title: Integer Overflow in Isolation Mode Debt Calculation
rejected_candidates.reason: False positive. Solidity 0.8 checked arithmetic plus `SafeCast` and bounded debt-ceiling storage prevent silent truncation/overflow here.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing Validation for Debt Ceiling in Isolation Mode
rejected_candidates.reason: A large debt ceiling chosen by governance is a risk-parameter decision, not a code vulnerability by itself.
